name: Modified Binary Search
summary: Binary search where, at each midpoint, one side is guaranteed sorted; decide whether the target lies in that sorted side and discard the other half.
visualizationIntent: init: the full array is the active window with left at the start and right at the end. update: mid is computed and highlighted as current. check: the code decides which half is sorted and whether the target lives there. move: the window collapses to one half, dimming the discarded cells. match: when nums[mid] equals target the cell turns result-green and the index is returned. return: on an exhausted window the search reports -1.

lineExplanations.3: Start the left boundary at the first index.
lineExplanations.4: Start the right boundary at the last index.
lineExplanations.5: Keep searching while the window still contains at least one element.
lineExplanations.6: Pick the middle index of the current window.
lineExplanations.7: Check whether the middle element is the target.
lineExplanations.8: Return the middle index because the target was found.
lineExplanations.9: Decide whether the left half of the window is the sorted one.
lineExplanations.10: In a sorted left half, check whether the target lies inside it.
lineExplanations.11: Discard the right half by moving the right boundary left of mid.
lineExplanations.13: Otherwise discard the left half by moving the left boundary right of mid.
lineExplanations.15: In a sorted right half, check whether the target lies inside it.
lineExplanations.16: Discard the left half by moving the left boundary right of mid.
lineExplanations.18: Otherwise discard the right half by moving the right boundary left of mid.
lineExplanations.19: Return -1 because the window emptied without finding the target.

syntaxExplanations.1: class Solution: defines the class wrapper expected by the platform.
syntaxExplanations.2: def search(self, nums, target): defines the method receiving nums and target.
syntaxExplanations.3: left = 0 sets the left boundary to the first index.
syntaxExplanations.4: right = len(nums) - 1 sets the right boundary to the last index.
syntaxExplanations.5: while left <= right: repeats while the window is non-empty.
syntaxExplanations.6: mid = (left + right) // 2 computes the middle index using integer division.
syntaxExplanations.7: if nums[mid] == target: is true when the middle value equals the target.
syntaxExplanations.8: return mid sends back the found index and stops.
syntaxExplanations.9: if nums[left] <= nums[mid]: is true when the left half is sorted.
syntaxExplanations.10: if nums[left] <= target and target < nums[mid]: tests whether target is inside the sorted left half.
syntaxExplanations.11: right = mid - 1 shrinks the window to the left half.
syntaxExplanations.12: else: runs when the target is not in the sorted left half.
syntaxExplanations.13: left = mid + 1 shrinks the window to the right half.
syntaxExplanations.14: else: runs when the right half is the sorted one.
syntaxExplanations.15: if nums[mid] < target and target <= nums[right]: tests whether target is inside the sorted right half.
syntaxExplanations.16: left = mid + 1 shrinks the window to the right half.
syntaxExplanations.17: else: runs when the target is not in the sorted right half.
syntaxExplanations.18: right = mid - 1 shrinks the window to the left half.
syntaxExplanations.19: return -1 reports that the target is absent.
